data.title: Goldman cools on Target as discretionary spending slows
data.description:
With a product mix more heavily skewed towards discretionary items, Goldman Sachs sees Target (NYSE:TGT) more vulnerable to a decline in consumer spending compared to peers like Walmart (WMT), BJ’s Wholesale Club (BJ), and Costco (COST). And with a recovery in growth for discretionary categories in the near term less likely, Goldman moves to the sidelines on the retailer, downgrading Target (NYSE:TGT) to Neutral from Buy.

“In our view, there is more downside risk to EPS than upside given potential top line deleverage and risk of tariffs,” Goldman said in Wednesday’s research note, pointing to recent data from HundredX and Placer which shows Target’s (NYSE:TGT) sales may already be slowing as the macro environment grows more volatile.

Just as consumers were beginning to see a light at the end of the inflation tunnel, tariffs are taking another swing at spending habits.

“We’re asking consumers to play the long game, but from an inflation perspective, consumers have been playing the long game for years…they’re worn out,” said Matt Priest, CEO of Footwear Distributors and Retailers of America in a recent interview.

This leaves retailers like Target (TGT) with the hard choice of raising prices for an already price-sensitive consumer, or take a hit to margins, hoping that tariffs are only temporary.

And although spending around seasonal events will continue to plump up quarterly results, “the decline in overall discretionary spending will continue to weigh on Target’s top-line for the near term,” Goldman warns.  

For the time being, Goldman's Neutral outlook on Target (TGT) stands out among Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors, all of which currently share a consensus Buy rating for Target, albeit with a score of just 3.63-3.90. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating is more in-line with Goldman, rating the stock as a Hold with a Quant score of 3.04 out of 5.

Shares are down ~2% before Wednesday’s opening bell.

data.title: Progressive GAAP EPS of $4.37 misses by $0.35, revenue of $22.21B beats by $480M
data.description:
• Progressive press release (NYSE:PGR): Q1 GAAP EPS of $4.37 misses by $0.35.
  • Revenue of $22.21B (+17.1% Y/Y) beats by $480M.

data.title: Prologis Q1 earnings top consensus; dials back outlook on development starts, dispositions
data.description:
Prologis (NYSE:PLD) delivered stronger-than-expected Q1 earnings and revenue on Wednesday and reaffirmed its 2025 earnings guidance, but lowered expectations for dispositions and development starts as its customers become wary amid policy uncertainty.

The industrial REIT reaffirmed its guidance for 2025 core FFO per share at $5.65-$5.81, compared with the average analyst estimate of $5.75.

Spending on acquisitions was reaffirmed at $750M-$1.25B, but its outlook for dispositions was revised downward to $250M-$500M from its previous outlook of $1.00B-$1.50B. Development starts outlook was changed to $1.50B-$2.00B from $2.25B-$2.75B.

"In the near term, policy uncertainty is making customers more cautious," said co-founder and CEO Hamid R. Moghadam. "But over the long term, limited new supply and high construction costs support continued rent growth."

Q1 core FFO per share of $1.42, exceeding the consensus estimate of $1.38, decreased from $1.50 in Q4 2024 and increased from $1.28 in last year’s Q1.

Revenue of $2.14B, beating the $1.99B consensus estimate, fell from $2.20B in the previous quarter and rose from $1.96B a year ago.

Adjusted EBITDA of $1.77B slid from $2.11B in Q4 and climbed from $1.60B in Q1 2024.

Average occupancy dipped to 94.9% from 95.6% in Q4.

Cash same-store net operating income declined to 6.2% Y/Y vs. 6.7% growth in Q4.

Conference call at 12:00 PM ET.

data.title: PMGC Holdings to acquire a U.S.-based custom IT packaging company; shares fall
data.description:
• PMGC Holdings (NASDAQ:ELAB) announced on Wednesday the signing of a non-binding Letter of Intent to acquire a U.S.-based, cash-flow positive information technology custom packaging company.
  • Founded in 2011 and headquartered in Southern California, the target company is a provider of custom packaging solutions for IT products, serving over 300 commercial clients.
  • PMGC believes the target company has a compelling opportunity for scalable growth. PMGC believes that, with additional investment in marketing and the buildout of a dedicated sales team, the target company is well-positioned to unlock future commercial expansion. 
  • As part of the next phase of the acquisition and to remain compliant with SEC requirements, PMGC has initiated a financial audit of the target company. 
  • The company anticipates signing a definitive agreement and closing the transaction before the end of Q2, 2025. Upon closing, the target company will operate as a wholly owned subsidiary of PMGC Holdings. Its current operations team will remain in place.
  • Shares of PMGC Holdings (NASDAQ:ELAB) were down more than 18% in premarket trading.

data.title: Abbott slips after Q1 revenue miss; guidance reaffirmed
data.description:
Abbott Laboratories (NYSE:ABT) fell ~1% in the early premarket trading on Wednesday after the diagnostics company's Q1 2025 results fell short of Street forecasts for revenue, despite an earnings beat amid outperformances in its MedTech and Pharma divisions.

However, following in the footsteps of its larger rival, J&J (JNJ), on Tuesday, the North Chicago, Illinois-based healthcare giant reaffirmed its earnings outlook amid uncertainty over the Trump administration’s tariffs targeted at MedTech and Pharma products.  

"It is this diversification and execution that allows Abbott to navigate through periods of uncertainty and continually deliver sustainable growth,” CEO Robert Ford remarked ahead of the earnings call at 8 a.m. CST.

Abbott (NYSE:ABT) reported $10.36B in revenue for Q1 with ~4% YoY growth, missing the consensus by $60M as its Diagnostics segment underperformed, adding $2.1B to the topline with ~7% YoY drop compared to $2.2B in the consensus, according to Bloomberg data.

While the diagnostics segment expanded ~1% YoY excluding COVID-19 testing-related sales, the company attributed the sales contraction to the drop in COVID testing-related sales and the unfavorable impact of volume-based procurement programs in China.

Meanwhile, ABT's Established Pharmaceuticals unit and Nutrition segments expanded ~3% YoY and ~4% YoY growth with $1.3B and $2.1B in sales compared to $1.2B and $2.1B in the consensus, respectively.

ABT’s Diabetes Care segment powered its Medical Devices business to post $4.90B in sales for the quarter with ~10% YoY growth, exceeding $4.85B in the consensus, as the company’s continuous glucose monitors added $1.7B with ~18% YoY growth.

Meanwhile, ABT’s adjusted operating margin showed a 130 bps improvement to reach ~21%, as the company reported $1.09 in adjusted earnings per share for the quarter with ~11% YoY growth, beating the consensus by $0.02.

The firm’s reaffirmed 2025 outlook indicates an adjusted operating margin of 23.5%—24.0% and adjusted earnings per share of $5.05—$5.25, compared to $5.15 in the consensus. Its full-year sales guidance for organic growth stands at 7.5%—8.5%.

data.title: Orbic North America partners with GCT Semiconductor to develop mobile hotspot, FWA gateway
data.description:
• Orbic North America and GCT Semiconductor (NYSE:GCTS) have signed a letter of intent for a partnership to jointly develop and supply an Orbic-branded mobile hotspot and FWA gateway utilizing a Verizon-certified 5G module based on GCT’s new 5G chipset.
  • As part of the collaborative multi-phase market rollout, Orbic and GCT will initially focus on devices for the Verizon network. Additionally, they will offer versions of the module and devices, including mobile hotspots and CPEs, to other network operators worldwide.
  • GCT will support the joint development and technical efforts by providing core services, including technical expertise, and chipsets for volume production requirements.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Travelers Q1 earnings beat consensus as underwriting helps offset catastrophe losses
data.description:
The Travelers Companies (NYSE:TRV) stock rose 2.1% in Wednesday premarket trading after the insurer boosted its quarterly cash dividend and turned in Q1 earnings that were supported by strong underlying underwriting income, even as its catastrophe losses surged due to the California wildfires in January.

The company increased its regular quarterly dividend by 5% to $1.10 per share.

Q4 core EPS of $1.91, beating the average analyst estimate of $0.77, fell from $9.15 in Q4 2024 and $4.69 in last year’s Q1.

Revenue of $11.8B, topping the $11.0B consensus, declined from $12.0B in the previous quarter and increased from $11.2B a year ago.

Net earned premiums in property and casualty operations dropped to $10.7B from $10.9B in Q4 and rose from $10.1B in Q1 2024.

Net investment income, pretax, of $930M fell from $955M in the prior quarter and surged from $84M in the year-ago period.

Combined ratio jumped to 102.5% from 83.2% in Q4 and 93.9% in last year’s Q1. Underlying combined ratio of 84.8% increased from 84.0% in the prior quarter and fell from 87.7% a year ago.

Catastrophe losses, pretax, increased to $2.27B, primarily driven by the January California wildfires, from $175M in Q4 and $712M in Q1 2024.

Pretax underlying underwriting income of $1.58B fell from $1.70B in Q4 and rose from $1.20B in last year's Q1.

Adjusted book value per share of $138.99 at March 31, 2025, dipped from $139.04 at Dec. 31, 2024 and climbed from $125.53 at March 31, 2024.

Conference call at 9:00 AM ET.

Earlier, The Travelers Companies Non-GAAP EPS of $1.91, net written premiums of $10.52B misses by $450M

data.title: United Rentals is called a tariff-resistant top pick by Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Morgan Stanley turned bullish on United Rentals (NYSE:URI) on Wednesday with an upgrade to an Overweight from Equal-weight. 

The MS analyst team remained bearish on the overall non-residential construction landscape, but likes United Rentals (NYSE:URI) for a number of reasons. 

United Rentals (URI) was said to have relatively limited direct exposure to tariff headwinds, which combined with the company's strong pricing power over suppliers is seen positioning it to better mitigate the risk of inflation. The company's ability to manage free cash flow via capex during economic downturns was also caused a positive because it can help preserve healthy leverage and liquidity levels. 

In addition, the firm said the equipment business is largely fungible, allowing United Rentals (URI) to move its fleet to areas of the country that may be performing better and away from areas that are more challenged. On a broad scale, steady and longer term secular tailwinds from higher rental penetration into the overall construction equipment market are expected to boost URI.

United Rentals' (URI) strong balance sheet and disciplined capital allocation position is anticipated to be able to weather the storm and perhaps even allow the company able to take advantage of any value opportunities such as bolt-on acquisitions or buybacks.

Morgan Stanley assigned a price target to URI of $702, which is 8X the FY25 EBITDA estimate of $7.17 billion.

Shares of United Rentals (URI) are down 15.5% on a year-to-date basis.
